articles.title: A Cookie for Dario? — Anthropic and selling death
articles.summary: A big tech headline this week is Anthropic (makers of Claude, widely regarded as one of the best LLM platforms) resisting Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth’s calls to modify their platform in order to enable it to support his commission of war crimes. As has become clear this week, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has declined to do so. The administration couches the request as an attempt to use the technology for “lawful purposes”, but given that they’ve also described their recent crimes as legal, t

articles.title: Why Apple’s move to video could endanger podcasting's greatest power
articles.summary: TL;DR: Apple is adding support for video podcasts to their podcast app Podcasts are built on an open standard, which is why they aren’t controlled by a bad algorithm and don’t have ads that spy on you Apple’s new system for video podcasts breaks with the old podcast standard, and forces creators to host their video clips with a few selected companies The stakes are even higher because all the indie video infrastructure companies have been bought by private equity, while Trump’s goons go after TV

articles.title: Separating AI hype from engineering reality
articles.summary: Why leadership, not models, determines AI impact. The post Separating AI hype from engineering reality appeared first on LeadDev.

articles.title: Interactive explanations
articles.summary: Agentic Engineering Patterns > When we lose track of how code written by our agents works we take on cognitive debt. For a lot of things this doesn't matter: if the code fetches some data from a database and outputs it as JSON the implementation details are likely simple enough that we don't need to care. We can try out the new feature and make a very solid guess at how it works, then glance over the code to be sure. Often though the details really do matter. If the core of our application be
